skip_reason: statement_timeout=8s hit for BM25 path on structured/non-semantic lesson texts (delivery reports, task IDs, Russian task summaries) — these texts cause full BM25 index scan; excluded from overlap stats to avoid bias

corpus.source: random sample of active lessons with embeddings, excluding '[DOC:' and '[WATCHDOG]' prefixes and texts <80 chars (live corpus)

claim_correction.prior_claim: median overlap@10 vs recall_hybrid = 90% (analytical estimate re-stamped from v0.9.8 MuSiQue controlled corpus, commit c3b332c — unverified label in code_review 9250)
claim_correction.measured_value: 80% (0.800) — confirmed by two independent live-corpus runs (30-query primary + 80-query supplemental)
claim_correction.significance: Material correction but gate still PASS (threshold >=70%). 90% was estimated on MuSiQue controlled corpus (2500 rows, sparse edges). Live prod_corpus has 73258 mem_edges causing BM25+graph divergence. v0.10.0.json recall_ranking_impact field corrected to reflect 80%.

supplemental_run_80q.description: Supplemental 80-query run on project_id=9 (5718 lessons, 73258 mem_edges), 2026-06-21
supplemental_run_80q.note: Timeout rate in supplemental run (10s cap) is 13.8%; primary run (8s cap) was 27% — different timeout thresholds + corpus filter (project_id=9 only) explain rate difference. Both confirm recall_hybrid is unreliable as default path on dense-graph corpora.

concerns_for_release_decision: HYBRID TIMEOUT RATE: 13.8-27% of queries cause recall_hybrid to timeout (8-10s threshold). Root cause: recursive graph_walk CTE over 73258 mem_edges (73339 temporal + 919 causal) in prod_corpus. recall_hybrid is unsafe as MCP default on production corpora with dense temporal/causal edge graphs. recall_fast as default is CONFIRMED correct by this finding.
HYBRID TAIL LATENCY: p95=2118ms for successful hybrid calls in 80-query run. Even queries that complete show extreme tail latency — unusable for interactive/MCP use regardless of timeout rate.

overlap@10 p10 = 0.40 — worst 10% of queries have <40% overlap: for those queries recall_fast misses 60%+ of what recall_hybrid would find. Agent callers who depend on high recall for safety-critical lookups should use deep=True.
overlap@1 p10 = 0.00 — for some queries fast and hybrid agree on zero top-1 items: callers relying on top-1 for high-precision single-match (e.g. exact-fact retrieval) should use deep=True.
Corpus sample is biased toward Russian-language task delivery summaries (majority of agent_lesson corpus in this DB). Real-world EN semantic lesson queries may show different overlap distribution.
methodology: Primary run: overlap@K = |recall_fast top-K ∩ recall_hybrid top-K| / K measured over 22 completed queries (30 sampled, 8 skipped due to BM25 8s timeout) on live prod_corpus corpus (7612 lessons with embeddings). Queries are random active lesson embeddings reused as query vectors; lesson_text used as BM25 query for hybrid. recall_fast: HNSW cosine ORDER BY <=> LIMIT k only. recall_hybrid: HNSW + BM25 RRF (vec_weight=0.4, bm25_weight=0.4, rrf_k=60). No role/project filter (global corpus). Benchmark script: benchmarks/scripts/bench_recall_fast_vs_hybrid_v010.py. Supplemental run: 80 queries, project_id=9, k=1/5/10/20 for recall_fast + k=10 for recall_hybrid (10s timeout).
note: overlap@K = |recall_fast top-K ∩ recall_hybrid top-K| / K. Measures recall@K cost of dropping graph_walk + BM25 RRF (fast path). Threshold 0.70 = acceptable quality for interactive/MCP use-case. fast_latency < 200ms = interactive latency target for MCP default path.
verification: Two independent live benchmark runs on prod_corpus (pgmnemo v0.10.0, 2026-06-21). Primary: 30 queries, 22 successful hybrid results, k=1/5/10/20. Supplemental: 80 queries, 69/80 hybrid k=10 successful (10s timeout). Both runs confirm overlap@10=0.80. Gate PASS. Corrects unverified 90% claim from commit c3b332c.
